programme.desc: Coverage from the women's singles final, which take place at Stade Roland Garros in Paris. America's Coco Gauff claimed just her second ever Major title last year after she beat Belarus' Aryna Sabalenka 6-7, 6-2, 6-4 in the final. That ended her two-year wait for a Major after her first at the US Open back in 2023. Sabalenka missed out on her fourth Grand Slam title but did go on to win the US Open later on in 2025.

programme.title: Live: Roland Garros
programme.desc: Coverage from the men's singles final, which take place at Stade Roland Garros in Paris. Last year's final saw Spain's Carlos Alcaraz fight back from two sets down to beat Jannik Sinner and claim his second successive French Open title. Sinner had won the first two sets 6-4 and 7-6 but the tide turned in the third set as Alcaraz won 6-4. The final two sets both went to tie-breaks before the Spaniard won them to win his fifth Major title.

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.sub-title: India v Afghanistan-Test Match, Day One
programme.desc: India v Afghanistan. Coverage of the first day of the one-off Test from Maharaja Yadavindra Singh PCA Stadium in Punjab. This is the first meeting between these two sides in this format since 2018 and only the second ever Test match involving the teams. The last meeting saw India make 474 in their only innings, before they bowled Afghanistan out for 109. The visitors then followed on and were bowled out for just 103 as India won by an innings and 262 runs.

programme.title: US Open 2013: Nadal v Djokovic
programme.desc: Revisit the 2013 US Open final as Rafael Nadal overpowered Novak Djokovic to win a second Flushing Meadows title.

programme.title: US Open 2019: Nadal v Medvedev
programme.desc: Rafael Nadal won his 19th Grand Slam and fourth US Open title with victory over Daniil Medvedev in 2019 at Flushing Meadows.

programme.title: Rome 2005: Nadal v Coria
programme.desc: In the 2005 Rome final, Rafael Nadal and Guillermo Coria went back and forth for over five hours in a breathtaking display of raw physicality and determination across five epic sets.

programme.title: Monte Carlo 2006: Nadal v Federer
programme.desc: Rafael Nadal and Roger Federer met in the first of three consecutive Monte-Carlo Masters finals, as the Spaniard overcame the then world number one Federer in four thrilling sets.

programme.title: Rome 2006: Nadal v Federer
programme.desc: In a five-hour final at the Foro Italico in Rome, Rafael Nadal refused to succumb to Roger Federer as one of tennis' great rivalries came to a head.

programme.title: ATP/WTA Tour Classic Matches
programme.desc: In a three-hour test of wills, Justine Henin was bidding to close an up-and-down year with the defence of her WTA Finals title against Maria Sharapova in 2007.

programme.title: Murray's Moments
programme.desc: With a second Wimbledon title and Olympic gold in Rio in the bank, Andy Murray capped 2016 by capturing world-number-one status after beating Novak Djokovic at the ATP World Tour Finals.

programme.title: 2014 Djokovic v Federer
